threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 243 bus runs on time. It’s like playing a game of hide and seek with the TfL app.
Got on the 59 last night, and there was a bloke strumming a ukulele. Half the bus joined in for 'Wonderwall'—Brixton at its finest.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using the bus shelter as a stage for their breakdancing battle while commuters just shrugged and smiled.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how some Kebab shops here serve the döner with fries instead of salad? It's a Berlin thing that locals swear by!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone's a local when they can pinpoint the exact time of day the BART delays are most likely to ruin their plans. It's a science.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy calmly eating a burrito while the bus lurched to a halt. He didn’t even flinch, just kept on chomping like it was all part of the ride.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the human towers are just for selfies, but locals see them as a deep-rooted tradition, a real test of community strength.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still look at their map while standing on the corner of Passeig de Gràcia, oblivious to the locals whizzing by.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a bus stop is just a place to wait, not a lively social hub with its own vibe and gossip.
Caught a late 333 last night; driver made an impromptu stop just to let a group of salsa dancers off near Coldharbour. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid on a skateboard dodging a protest march while eating a Döner, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Kreuzberg's street art isn't just decoration; it's a timeline of social movements and protests, with each mural telling a hidden story of the neighborhood.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're from here if they still shake their head at the Muni delays but act like missing a bus is no big deal—as long as they’re not waiting at Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the #38 stuck behind a parade of dogs outside Civic Center this morning. Only in SF do you see a furry traffic jam like that.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think tapas are all about food, but locals know it’s really about the conversations that last till the early hours.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their over-enthusiastic nodding at the buskers on every corner, as if they think it’s a personal concert just for them.
